meta.title: Menopause Symptom Calculator — MRS Score Online

menopauseSymptom.title: Menopause Symptom Calculator
menopauseSymptom.description: Score your menopause symptoms using the clinically validated Menopause Rating Scale (MRS) — 11 symptoms, 3 domains, clear next steps.
menopauseSymptom.intro: Rate how strongly you currently experience each of the following symptoms:
menopauseSymptom.sleepProblems: Sleep problems (falling asleep, staying asleep, early waking)
menopauseSymptom.exhaustion: Physical and mental exhaustion (performance drop, forgetfulness)
menopauseSymptom.interpretation_none: No relevant menopause symptoms right now. Track changes and re-check if your experience shifts.
menopauseSymptom.interpretation_mild: Mild menopause symptoms. Lifestyle measures (sleep hygiene, exercise, nutrition, stress reduction) often help noticeably.
menopauseSymptom.interpretation_moderate: Moderate symptoms that may affect daily life. A focused gynecology consultation about treatment options is reasonable.
menopauseSymptom.interpretation_severe: Severe symptoms with clear quality-of-life impact. A specialist work-up with a structured treatment plan (e.g. hormone therapy, local estrogen, phytotherapy) is clearly recommended.
menopauseSymptom.evaluationRecommended: Specialist evaluation recommended — book a gynecology appointment.
menopauseSymptom.evaluationNotRecommended: No urgent specialist evaluation indicated. Re-check if symptoms worsen.
menopauseSymptom.mrsTitle: What is the Menopause Rating Scale?
menopauseSymptom.mrsText: The MRS is the most widely used international questionnaire for assessing menopause symptoms (Heinemann et al. 2003). It captures 11 typical symptoms across 3 dimensions — somato-vegetative, psychological and urogenital — yielding a total score 0–44 plus subscale scores.
menopauseSymptom.somaticDescription: Hot flushes, heart discomfort, sleep problems, joint/muscle complaints
menopauseSymptom.urogenitalDescription: Sexual problems, bladder symptoms, vaginal dryness
menopauseSymptom.howItWorksText: Rate each of the 11 symptoms on a 0 (none) to 4 (very severe) scale. The calculator sums your answers into a total score (0–44) and reports a severity band per dimension. Cutoffs follow the original validation (Heinemann 2003, Schneider 2000) and are used worldwide in research and practice.
menopauseSymptom.disclaimer: This calculator is a self-screening tool, not a diagnostic instrument. Treatment decisions (e.g. hormone therapy) always belong with your gynecologist.

faq.q: What is the Menopause Rating Scale (MRS)?
faq.a: The MRS is an internationally validated 11-item questionnaire developed in 1996 to score menopause symptoms. It is translated into 25+ languages. Heinemann et al. 2003 established the severity cutoffs in use today.

faq.q: Which symptoms does the MRS capture?
faq.a: 11 typical symptoms across 3 domains: somato-vegetative (hot flushes, heart, sleep, joints), psychological (mood, irritability, anxiety, exhaustion) and urogenital (sexuality, bladder, vaginal dryness).

faq.q: What does my total score mean?
faq.a: 0–4: none to very mild. 5–8: mild. 9–16: moderate — consult your gynecologist. 17+: severe — therapy is usually clearly indicated.

faq.q: When should I see a gynecologist?
faq.a: Once symptoms are moderate (score 9+) or if a single domain (e.g. urogenital) stands out. With severe symptoms (17+) or bleeding changes a work-up is clearly indicated — also to rule out other causes.

faq.q: What treatments exist for menopause symptoms?
faq.a: Lifestyle (exercise, sleep hygiene, cool rooms, weight), hormone therapy (HT) systemic or local, non-hormonal options (SSRI/SNRI, gabapentin, clonidine), phytotherapy (black cohosh, soy isoflavones) and cognitive behavioral therapy for hot flushes.

faq.q: How long do menopause symptoms last?
faq.a: Perimenopause typically begins in the mid-40s and lasts 4–8 years. Vasomotor symptoms (hot flushes) persist a median of 7.4 years — for some women more than 14 years. Urogenital symptoms often remain lifelong without local treatment.
